summary: Matched the common fighter HammerFall code by replacing 204 lines of assembly in asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s with a new C translation in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerFall.c and header. The matched file reports 692 bytes at 100.00%, covering ftCo_800C5CD4, ftCo_800C5D34, ftCo_HammerFall_Anim, ftCo_800C5DDC, ftCo_HammerFall_IASA, ftCo_HammerFall_Phys, and ftCo_HammerFall_Coll. The PR also moved the HammerFall declarations out of ft_0C31.h into a dedicated ftCo_HammerFall.h, added the C object to obj_files.mk, updated dependent common fighter files to include the new header, typed ftCo_800C5F88 as taking ftCo_GObj*, and added a pl_8003FDF4 prototype needed by the translation.
what_changed: Removed the HammerFall assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s, including the seven matched routines and two local sdata2 constants that were no longer needed there.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerFall.c implementing the HammerFall transition helper, motion-state setup, animation callback, IASA callback, physics callback, collision callback, and a shared static inline helper for the hammer/item drop behavior.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerFall.h with address-commented prototypes for ftCo_800C5CD4, ftCo_800C5D34, ftCo_HammerFall_Anim, ftCo_800C5DDC, ftCo_HammerFall_IASA, ftCo_HammerFall_Phys, and ftCo_HammerFall_Coll.
Added ftCo_HammerFall.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31.s object, preserving object order while moving the matched code to C.
Updated ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_DamageFall.c, ftCo_HammerJump.c, ftCo_HammerWait.c, and ftCo_Init.c to include ftCo_HammerFall.h instead of relying on declarations from the broader ft_0C31.h.
Removed HammerFall prototypes from src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h and refined the remaining ftCo_800C5F88 declaration from placeholder-style UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftCo_800C5F88(ftCo_GObj* gobj).
Added the missing pl_8003FDF4(u8, int) prototype to src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h for the call made by the matched HammerFall helper.
smart_moves: Split the newly matched state into a dedicated ftCo_HammerFall.c/.h pair rather than leaving declarations in the catch-all ft_0C31.h, making dependencies more local and searchable.

Used thin wrappers for simple callbacks: ftCo_HammerFall_Anim delegates to ftCo_800C4F64, ftCo_HammerFall_Phys delegates to ft_80084DB0, and ftCo_HammerFall_Coll delegates to ft_80082C74 with ftCo_800C5F88.

Factored the repeated item/bonus side-effect sequence from ftCo_800C5DDC and ftCo_HammerFall_IASA into a static inline helper named inlineA0, matching both the bool-returning helper and the void IASA callback without duplicating code.
Captured a matching-sensitive ordering constraint in a source comment: the flags variable in ftCo_800C5D34 cannot move below the Fighter* fp declaration, suggesting an inline or ordering dependency.
Improved a nearby unknown prototype for ftCo_800C5F88 after isolating the HammerFall portion, reducing placeholder usage in ft_0C31.h.
decomp_lessons: When a matched subset is extracted from a large assembly file, keep the new C object adjacent to the old asm object in obj_files.mk so the remaining assembly continues to occupy the expected link position.
For common fighter states, a dedicated state header is preferable once the state is decompiled; dependent files should include the specific ftCo_HammerFall.h rather than a broad numeric header.
Identical assembly sequences used by a bool helper and a void callback can often be represented with a static inline side-effect helper plus a return value in only the bool wrapper.

Local variable declaration order can be part of the matching strategy. The explicit comment in ftCo_800C5D34 records that moving the flags variable changes codegen and may imply an inlined helper or compiler scheduling sensitivity.
Replacing placeholder prototypes is a useful opportunistic cleanup during matching, but should be evidence-grounded to nearby call signatures; this PR types ftCo_800C5F88 as void taking ftCo_GObj*.
naming_conventions: State callback names follow the ftCo_HammerFall_* convention for Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
Unidentified helper functions retain address-based names such as ftCo_800C5CD4, ftCo_800C5D34, and ftCo_800C5DDC.
The new header uses an address-based include guard, GALE01_0C5CD4, matching the start address of the newly extracted block.
Prototypes in the new header retain address comments like /* 0C5CD4 */ for symbol-to-address searchability.
The temporary matching helper is named inlineA0, signaling that its exact semantic name was not established and that it exists primarily to express matching structure.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ftCo_800C5CD4 as a boolean transition gate checking ftCo_800C5240(gobj) and fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_AB before calling ftCo_800C5D34.
Matched ftCo_800C5D34 by storing ftCo_800C54C4(fp) into a separate u32 flags variable before calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_HammerFall, flags, ftCo_800C548C(fp), 1, 0, NULL).
Preserved the assembly's ground check by calling ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp) when fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground, then calling ftCo_800C4E94(fp).
Represented the zeroed stack Vec3 used for Item_8026ABD8 with pos.x = pos.y = pos.z = 0, followed by Item_8026ABD8(fp->item_gobj, &pos, 1).
Expressed the input and timer gate in ftCo_800C5DDC as fp->x2338.y != 0, HSD_PAD_A in fp->input.x668, HSD_PAD_LR in fp->input.held_inputs, and fp->x683 >= p_ftCommonData->x1C.
Kept sdata2 constants implicit through literal arguments 1, 0, and NULL in C, allowing removal of ftCo_804D8D98 and ftCo_804D8D9C from the remaining assembly block.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit human-facing note in the diff is an author TODO in ftCo_800C5D34: 'Cannot move below fp, which suggests an inline', documenting a matching constraint rather than reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type ftCo_800C5CD4, ftCo_800C5D34, ftCo_800C5DDC, or inlineA0.
Search for other Hammer state files that use the same pattern of dedicated ftCo_Hammer*.c/.h extraction from ft_0C31.s.
Check later work around ftCo_800C5F88 and HammerLanding to see whether the remaining ft_0C31.s code was matched using the typed ftCo_GObj* prototype.
Look for PRs touching pl_8003FDF4 or Item_8026ABD8 to confirm the semantics of the hammer/item side-effect helper.
Search for other matching comments mentioning variable order or suspected inline constraints to reuse the declaration-order tactic.
